title: Customize your maps
code: pd <- copy(csmaps::nor_county_map_b2020_insert_oslo_dt)  q <- ggplot() q <- q + geom_polygon(   data = pd,    mapping = aes(     x = long,      y = lat,     group = group,     fill = location_code   ),     color="black",   size=0.1 ) q <- q + annotate(   "text",   x = csmaps::nor_xxx_position_title_insert_oslo_b2020_insert_oslo_dt$long,   y = csmaps::nor_xxx_position_title_insert_oslo_b2020_insert_oslo_dt$lat,   label = "Oslo" ) q <- q + theme_void() q <- q + coord_quickmap() q <- q + labs(title = "") q

title: Customize your maps
text: also possible specify color user-defined groups. show example assigning different (pseudo) risk level county.
code: pd <- copy(csmaps::nor_county_map_b2020_insert_oslo_dt)  # assign each location a random category for different colors location_info <- unique(pd[,c("location_code")]) location_info[,category:=rep(   c("Good","Normal","Neutral","Bad","Very Bad"),   each=3)[1:.N] ] location_info[,category:=factor(   category,   levels=c("Good","Normal","Neutral","Bad","Very Bad")   ) ] print(location_info) #>     location_code category #>  1:  county_nor11     Good #>  2:  county_nor15     Good #>  3:  county_nor18     Good #>  4:  county_nor03   Normal #>  5:  county_nor30   Normal #>  6:  county_nor34   Normal #>  7:  county_nor38  Neutral #>  8:  county_nor42  Neutral #>  9:  county_nor46  Neutral #> 10:  county_nor50      Bad #> 11:  county_nor54      Bad  # join the map data.table pd[location_info,on="location_code",category:=category]  q <- ggplot() q <- q + geom_polygon(   data = pd,    mapping = aes(     x = long,     y = lat,     group = group,     fill=category   ),    color="black",    size=0.25 ) q <- q + annotate(   "text",   x = csmaps::nor_xxx_position_title_insert_oslo_b2020_insert_oslo_dt$long,   y = csmaps::nor_xxx_position_title_insert_oslo_b2020_insert_oslo_dt$lat,   label = "Oslo" ) q <- q + coord_quickmap() q <- q + labs(title="") q <- q + theme_void() q

title: Customize your maps
text: can add labels county index onto maps. several options adding texts graph ggplot2. recommend geom_label() add labels label overlap occurs, otherwise recommend using ggrepel::geom_label_repel().  Labels can easily added layouts, Oslo wards.
code: pd <- copy(csmaps::nor_county_map_b2020_insert_oslo_dt) q <- ggplot() q <- q + geom_polygon(   data = pd,    mapping = aes(     x = long,     y = lat,     group = group,     fill = location_code   ),     color="black",   size=0.1 ) q <- q + annotate(   "text",   x = csmaps::nor_xxx_position_title_insert_oslo_b2020_insert_oslo_dt$long,   y = csmaps::nor_xxx_position_title_insert_oslo_b2020_insert_oslo_dt$lat,   label = "Oslo" ) q <- q + geom_label(   data = csmaps::nor_county_position_geolabels_b2020_default_dt,   mapping = aes(x = long, y = lat, label = location_code)   ) # ggrepel::geom_label_repel() for avoiding overlap q <- q + theme_void() q <- q + coord_quickmap() q <- q + labs(title = "") q q <- ggplot(mapping = aes(x = long, y = lat)) q <- q + geom_polygon(   data = csmaps::oslo_ward_map_b2020_default_dt,   mapping = aes(group = group),   color = "black",   fill = "white",   size = 0.2 ) q <- q + geom_label(   data = csmaps::oslo_ward_position_geolabels_b2020_default_dt,   mapping = aes(label = location_code),   color = "red",   size = 3,   label.size = 0.1,   label.r = grid::unit(0, "lines") ) q <- q + theme_void() q <- q + coord_quickmap() q

title: Customize your maps
text: convenient use csdata package enrich Norway Oslo maps external information, location name population. illustrate .

what: Add county name and population to Norway map
title: Customize your maps
code:
# enrich with population and location name dpop_2020 <- csdata::nor_population_by_age_cats()[calyear==2020]  # join, create label labels <- copy(csmaps::nor_county_position_geolabels_b2020_insert_oslo_dt) labels[   dpop_2020,    on = "location_code",   pop_total := pop_jan1_n ] labels[   csdata::nor_locations_names(),    on = "location_code",   location_name := location_name ] labels[, label := paste0(location_name, '
', pop_total)] print(head(labels)) #>    location_code     long      lat pop_total        location_name #> 1:  county_nor30  8.85000 60.60000   1241165                Viken #> 2:  county_nor03 10.72028 59.98000    693494                 Oslo #> 3:  county_nor34 11.00000 61.86886    371385            Innlandet #> 4:  county_nor38  8.50000 59.32481    419396 Vestfold og Telemark #> 5:  county_nor42  7.80000 58.30000    307231                Agder #> 6:  county_nor11  6.10000 58.70000    479892             Rogaland #>                           label #> 1:               Viken
1241165 #> 2:                 Oslo
693494 #> 3:            Innlandet
371385 #> 4: Vestfold og Telemark
419396 #> 5:                Agder
307231 #> 6:             Rogaland
479892  # plot pd <- copy(csmaps::nor_county_map_b2020_insert_oslo_dt) q <- ggplot() q <- q + geom_polygon(   data = pd,    mapping = aes(     x = long,     y = lat,     group = group,     fill = location_code   ),   color="black",   size=0.1 ) q <- q + annotate(   "text",   x = csmaps::nor_xxx_position_title_insert_oslo_b2020_insert_oslo_dt$long,   y = csmaps::nor_xxx_position_title_insert_oslo_b2020_insert_oslo_dt$lat,   label = "Oslo" ) q <- q + ggrepel::geom_label_repel(   data = labels,   mapping = aes(x = long, y = lat, label = label) ) q <- q + theme_void() q <- q + coord_quickmap() q <- q + labs(title = "") q

what: Add location name for ward and population for Oslo map
title: Customize your maps
code:
# enrich with population and location name dpop_2020 <- csdata::nor_population_by_age_cats()[calyear==2020]  # join, create label labels <- copy(csmaps::oslo_ward_position_geolabels_b2020_default_dt) labels[   dpop_2020,    on = "location_code",   pop_total := pop_jan1_n ] labels[   csdata::nor_locations_names(),    on = "location_code",   location_name := location_name ] labels[, label := paste0(location_name, '
', pop_total)] print(head(labels)) #>         location_code     long      lat pop_total  location_name #> 1: wardoslo_nor030101 10.79760 59.91010     58671     Gamle Oslo #> 2: wardoslo_nor030102 10.78000 59.92567     62423    Grünerløkka #> 3: wardoslo_nor030103 10.76683 59.93981     45089         Sagene #> 4: wardoslo_nor030104 10.73555 59.91230     38945 St. Hanshaugen #> 5: wardoslo_nor030105 10.66500 59.89925     59269        Frogner #> 6: wardoslo_nor030106 10.65000 59.92500     34569         Ullern #>                    label #> 1:     Gamle Oslo
58671 #> 2:    Grünerløkka
62423 #> 3:         Sagene
45089 #> 4: St. Hanshaugen
38945 #> 5:        Frogner
59269 #> 6:         Ullern
34569  q <- ggplot(mapping = aes(x = long, y = lat)) q <- q + geom_polygon(   data = csmaps::oslo_ward_map_b2020_default_dt,   mapping = aes(group = group),   color = "black",   fill = "white",   size = 0.2 ) q <- q + geom_label(   data = labels,   mapping = aes(label = label),   color = "red",   size = 3,   label.size = 0.1,   label.r = grid::unit(0, "lines") ) q <- q + theme_void() q <- q + coord_quickmap() q

title: Layout
text: 3 layout options Norway map: default, split insert Oslo. Oslo county map, default layout.

title: Layout
code: pd <- copy(csmaps::nor_municip_map_b2020_default_dt) q <- ggplot() q <- q + geom_polygon(data = pd, aes( x = long, y = lat, group = group),                        color="black", fill="white", size=0.1) q <- q + theme_void() q <- q + coord_quickmap() q <- q + labs(title = "Default layout") q

title: Layout
code: pd <- copy(csmaps::nor_municip_map_b2020_split_dt) q <- ggplot() q <- q + geom_polygon(data = pd, aes( x = long, y = lat, group = group),                        color="black", fill="white", size=0.1) q <- q + theme_void() q <- q + coord_quickmap() q <- q + labs(title = "Split layout") q

title: Layout
code: pd <- copy(csmaps::nor_municip_map_b2020_insert_oslo_dt) q <- ggplot() q <- q + geom_polygon(data = pd, aes( x = long, y = lat, group = group),                        color="black", fill="white", size=0.1)  # add title "Oslo" q <- q + annotate(   "text",    x = csmaps::nor_xxx_position_title_insert_oslo_b2020_insert_oslo_dt$long,    y = csmaps::nor_xxx_position_title_insert_oslo_b2020_insert_oslo_dt$lat,   label = "Oslo"   ) q <- q + theme_void() q <- q + coord_quickmap() q <- q + labs(title = "Insert Oslo layout") q

previous_headings: Layout options > Norway layout: insert oslo
title: Layout
code: pd <- copy(csmaps::oslo_ward_map_b2020_default_dt) q <- ggplot() q <- q + geom_polygon(data = pd, aes(x = long, y = lat, group = group),                        color="black", fill="white", size=0.1) q <- q + theme_void() q <- q + coord_quickmap() q <- q + labs(title = "Oslo wards") q

title: Naming scheme
text: content map data contains. Currently focus 2 scopes: Country (Norway). City (Oslo).

title: Naming scheme
text: geographic granularity, defines border county, municipality ward. County (fylke) equivalent NUTS3 (Nomenclature Territorial Unit level 3). Municip (kommune) equivalent LAU2 (Local Administrative Unit level 2). Ward (bydel) administrative region within large municipality. xxx: used plotting position_title (see example).

title: Naming scheme
text: type data object distinguish whether ’s geographical map coordinate label positions. - Map: Map data. - Position geolabels: Geographical coordinates position labels, e.g. “county03” “Oslo”. - Position title (insert oslo): Geographical coordinate position title. far ’s layout: insert_oslo. (see example)

title: Naming scheme
text: Due recent redistricting, exist multiple versions county borders. provide maps match borders following years: 2020: current border, map contains 11 counties. 2019: Border redistricting 2020. map contains 18 counties. 2017: Border redistricting 2018. map contains 19 counties. information counties Norway can found .

title: Naming scheme
text: Map layout, see section examples.

title: Naming scheme
text: R class map object. data.table: applicable maps label/title coordinates. sf: simple feature spatial vector data. sf.

title: Interactive maps
text: provide option use sf class object create interactive maps. requres leaflet package.
code: pd_county <- csmaps::nor_county_map_b2020_default_sf leaflet::leaflet(   pd_county,   options = leaflet::leafletOptions(preferCanvas = F) ) %>%   leaflet::addProviderTiles(leaflet::providers$Esri.WorldGrayCanvas) %>%   leaflet::addPolygons(     weight = 0.3,     opacity = 1,     color = "black",      fillColor = "white",     fillOpacity = 0.9   )

title: Authors and Citation
text: package proudly brought : Richard Aubrey White. Author, maintainer. Chi Zhang. Author. Geonorge. Contributor. CSIDS. Copyright holder. See changelog contributors. 🙏

title: Authors and Citation
text: White RA, Zhang C (2022). csmaps: Preformatted Maps Norway Need Geolibraries. https://www.csids./csmaps/, https://github.com/csids/csmaps.
code: @Manual{,   title = {csmaps: Preformatted Maps of Norway that Don't Need Geolibraries},   author = {Richard Aubrey White and Chi Zhang},   year = {2022},   note = {https://www.csids.no/csmaps/, https://github.com/csids/csmaps}, }

title: Preformatted Maps of Norway that Don't Need Geolibraries
text: csmaps provides preformatted maps Norway generally need geolibraries. helps public health reporting purposes different geographic levels Norway. following geographic granularities included: County Municipality City ward (Oslo) convenient visualize maps additional information, either using text labels color palettes. Read introduction vignette run help(package="csmaps").

title: Preformatted Maps of Norway that Don't Need Geolibraries
text: csverse set R packages developed help solve problems frequently occur performing disease surveillance. want install dev versions (access packages haven’t released CRAN), run usethis::edit_r_profile() edit .Rprofile. write : Save file restart R. can now install csverse packages drat repository.
code: options(   repos = structure(c(     CSVERSE = "https://www.csids.no/drat/",     CRAN    = "https://cran.rstudio.com"   )) ) install.packages("csmaps")

code: # 2020 borders library(ggplot2) q <- ggplot(mapping = aes(x = long, y = lat)) q <- q + geom_polygon(   data = csmaps::nor_municip_map_b2020_insert_oslo_dt,   mapping = aes(group = group),   color = "black",   fill = "white",   size = 0.2 ) q <- q + annotate(   "text",   x = csmaps::nor_xxx_position_title_insert_oslo_b2020_insert_oslo_dt$long,   y = csmaps::nor_xxx_position_title_insert_oslo_b2020_insert_oslo_dt$lat,   label = "Oslo" ) q <- q + theme_void() q <- q + coord_quickmap() q   # 2019 borders library(ggplot2) q <- ggplot(mapping = aes(x = long, y = lat)) q <- q + geom_polygon(   data = csmaps::nor_municip_map_b2019_insert_oslo_dt,   mapping = aes(group = group),   color = "black",   fill = "white",   size = 0.2 ) q <- q + annotate(   "text",   x = csmaps::nor_xxx_position_title_insert_oslo_b2019_insert_oslo_dt$long,   y = csmaps::nor_xxx_position_title_insert_oslo_b2019_insert_oslo_dt$lat,   label = "Oslo" ) q <- q + theme_void() q <- q + coord_quickmap() q
